company.name: Alphabet

marketCap.rationale: Computed from Google's $85.00 IPO price and 271,219,643 total common shares outstanding after the offering in the final prospectus.

company.description: Search, ads, video, mobile, and cloud giant still centered economically on attention capture.

overview.title: The empire of default answers
paragraphs: Alphabet is a reminder that a search box can turn into an economic continent if enough adjacent products quietly feed it users, data, and ad inventory.

Unlike hardware-heavy empires, much of Alphabet's surface area is theoretically reproducible with open software and protocols. The challenge is that reproducing the data flywheel, distribution, and ad-market depth is harder than reproducing the software itself.

moatNarrative: Search quality, ad-market liquidity, distribution via Android and browsers, and YouTube's audience scale keep Alphabet resilient.
The moat is partly product quality and partly the advantage of already being where the world's queries and creators congregate.
decentralizationNarrative: Search aggregation, federated video, and de-Googled Android all create real cracks in the story that Google's stack is irreplaceable.
Still, open alternatives often struggle exactly where Google is strongest: defaults, reach, and advertiser convenience.
company.snapshotNote: Curated early-2026 public-source snapshot. Exact ordering can drift with the tape.

moat.rationale: Google's search and ad ecosystem is extremely entrenched, though less physically rooted than hardware moats.

decentralizability.rationale: Open search, federated media, and de-Googled mobile stacks create meaningful replacement paths.

profitability.rationale: Alphabet still turns defaults and ad infrastructure into enormous profit streams.

peRatio.rationale: Approximate valuation snapshot for a mature ad-and-cloud platform.

marketCap.rationale: Approximate market cap snapshot from public market trackers.

products.name: Google Search
products.summary: Alphabet's flagship query and ad-distribution surface.
products.whyItMatters: Search still funds a startling amount of Google's empire.
replacementSketch: Open metasearch and smaller search providers can disaggregate parts of the search experience.
Agentic AI may move value from traditional ranked pages toward multi-source synthesis layers.

alternatives.name: SearXNG
alternatives.summary: Open metasearch engine that lets operators own their search interface and aggregation logic.

decentralizationFit.rationale: Any operator can run an instance.

readiness.rationale: Useful now, though still not a drop-in replacement for Google's scale or data advantage.

costLeverage.rationale: Strong as a proof that search need not be singularly centralized.

openness.rationale: Strongly aligned with open search principles.

decentralizationFit.rationale: Distributed search is the explicit design goal.

readiness.rationale: Interesting but not mainstream or polished enough for general replacement.

costLeverage.rationale: More important as directional evidence than immediate competitive pressure.

products.name: YouTube
products.summary: Massive hosted video and creator platform with ad and subscription monetization.
products.whyItMatters: YouTube combines distribution, monetization, and default discoverability.
replacementSketch: Federated video and direct payment rails can reduce the need for a single platform to intermediate every creator-audience relationship.
AI-assisted subtitling, moderation, and discovery lower some of the operational excuses for centralization.

decentralizationFit.rationale: Directly attacks the assumption that video must live on one giant platform.

readiness.rationale: Strong for communities, not yet equal to YouTube's convenience or reach.

costLeverage.rationale: Useful pressure where creator communities can move with their audience.

alternatives.name: Nostr video clients
alternatives.summary: Protocol-native publishing tools with Bitcoin-adjacent identity and monetization possibilities.

openness.rationale: Protocol-centric and resistant to single-platform control.

decentralizationFit.rationale: The core premise is distributed publishing and identity.

readiness.rationale: Early and fragmented compared with mainstream creator platforms.

costLeverage.rationale: Strategically important even if still early.
